| Plugin and host use different struct definitions | ABI corruption          | Version and size checks                         |
| Passing ownership without allocator contract     | Heap mismatch           | Host-provided allocate/free or explicit destroy |
| Callback stored beyond host lifetime             | Dangling pointer        | Lifetime contract                               |
| Public `long` in ABI                             | data-model variation    | fixed-width types if needed                     |
| No compatibility test                            | Breaks plugins silently | ABI tests                                       |

### Case Study 4 — converting global-state code to context-object design

Old design:

```c id="p10_case_global_old"
static FILE *log_fp;
static int verbose;

void log_init(FILE *fp, int v)
{
    log_fp = fp;
    verbose = v;
}

void log_debug(const char *msg)
{
    if (verbose) {
        fprintf(log_fp, "%s\n", msg);
    }
}
```

Problems:

| Problem              | Consequence           |
| -------------------- | --------------------- |
| Global mutable state | not reentrant         |
| No null handling     | crash risk            |
| Hidden dependency    | hard to test          |
| Thread ambiguity     | races                 |
| Single instance only | inflexible            |
| No error return      | write failure ignored |

Context design:

```c id="p10_case_context_new"
struct logger {
    FILE *fp;
    int verbose;
};

int logger_init(struct logger *log, FILE *fp, int verbose)
{
    if (log == NULL || fp == NULL) {
        return -1;
    }

    log->fp = fp;
    log->verbose = verbose;
    return 0;
}

int logger_debug(struct logger *log, const char *msg)
{
    if (log == NULL || log->fp == NULL || msg == NULL) {
        return -1;
    }

    if (!log->verbose) {
        return 0;
    }

    return fprintf(log->fp, "%s\n", msg) < 0 ? -1 : 0;
}
```

Review:

| Improvement          | Result                                   |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Explicit state       | testable                                 |
| Multiple instances   | reentrant by design for distinct objects |
| Error return         | caller can handle I/O failure            |
| No hidden global     | clearer dependency                       |
| Future thread policy | can add lock inside context              |

### Case Study 5 — replacing unsafe string handling

Unsafe:

```c id="p10_case_string_bad"
char path[256];

strcpy(path, dir);
strcat(path, "/");
strcat(path, name);
```

Problems:

| Problem                       | Consequence                |
| ----------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| No capacity check             | overflow                   |
| Repeated scans                | inefficient                |
| Path separator assumption     | platform issue             |
| Silent truncation not handled | correctness/security       |
| Source strings assumed valid  | OOB read if not terminated |

Safer bounded builder:

```c id="p10_case_string_better"
int build_path(char *out, size_t cap,
               const char *dir,
               const char *name)
{
    int n;

    if (out == NULL || cap == 0 || dir == NULL || name == NULL) {
        return -1;
    }

    n = snprintf(out, cap, "%s/%s", dir, name);
    if (n < 0 || (size_t)n >= cap) {
        out[0] = '\0';
        return -1;
    }

    return 0;
}
```

This still assumes `/` is the intended separator and that `dir`/`name` are valid C strings. Cross-platform path handling needs a platform/path abstraction.

### Final Master Checklist — reading or writing any C function

| Category    | Questions                                                                  |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Inputs      | Are pointers nullable? Are buffers length-bounded? Are strings terminated? |
| Outputs     | Are output parameters initialized on failure? Who owns returned data?      |
| Ownership   | What does the function allocate, free, borrow, retain, take, or release?   |
| Lifetime    | Can any pointer outlive the object it points into?                         |
| Bounds      | Is every index/copy/write protected by length/capacity checks?             |
| Arithmetic  | Can size/range arithmetic overflow, underflow, or narrow?                  |
| Errors      | Are all fallible calls checked? Is error detail preserved where needed?    |
| Cleanup     | Are all resources released on every path?                                  |
| Invariants  | What must be true before and after the function?                           |
| Mutability  | What state can change? Is it visible from signature/name?                  |
| Aliasing    | Can parameters overlap? Is that allowed?                                   |
| Strings     | Is encoding, termination, and truncation policy defined?                   |
| Concurrency | Can another thread access the same object?                                 |
| Callbacks   | Are timing, lifetime, reentrancy, and thread rules documented?             |
| Portability | Is this ISO C or another layer?                                            |
| ABI         | Is any public binary layout/signature affected?                            |
| Tools       | What test/sanitizer/analyzer would catch likely bugs?                      |

### Final Master Checklist — reviewing any C module

| Category         | Questions                                               |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Public header    | Self-contained? Minimal? Correct includes?              |
| Names            | Project prefix? Ownership verbs clear?                  |
| Types            | Public value type or opaque type chosen deliberately?   |
| Lifecycle        | `init/deinit` or `create/destroy` consistent?           |
| Error convention | Uniform and documented?                                 |
| Resource policy  | Every owned field cleaned? Partial init safe?           |
| Internal linkage | Private helpers marked `static`?                        |
| Global state     | Avoided or justified? Thread-safe?                      |
| Platform code    | Isolated behind adapter?                                |
| Unsafe code      | Casts/raw memory centralized?                           |
| Tests            | Cover success, failure, edge cases, cleanup?            |
| Fuzzing          | Parser/boundary APIs fuzzable?                          |
| Sanitizers       | Module clean under ASan/UBSan where possible?           |
| ABI              | Public structs/symbols intentional?                     |
| Documentation    | Ownership, lifetime, nullability, thread safety stated? |

### Final Master Checklist — reviewing any C project

| Category        | Questions                                     |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Compiler policy | Which compilers and C standard modes?         |
| Build system    | Are dependencies modeled explicitly?          |
| Warnings        | Warning-clean? Which warnings?                |
| Sanitizers      | ASan/UBSan/TSan jobs?                         |
| Static analysis | Integrated or periodically run?               |
| Tests           | Unit, integration, regression, fuzz?          |
| CI matrix       | Matches support claims?                       |
| Portability     | POSIX/platform assumptions documented?        |
| Dependencies    | Versions, licenses, security updates tracked? |
| ABI policy      | Shared-library compatibility tested?          |
| Release process | Reproducible and documented?                  |
| Formatting      | Automated?                                    |
| Coding standard | Semantic rules, not only style?               |
| Documentation   | Build, API, porting, contributing docs?       |
| Security        | Vulnerability process and hardening policy?   |
| Performance     | Benchmarks and profiling discipline?          |

### High-Frequency C Failure Modes — final index

| Failure mode          | Root cause                             | Best prevention                     |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| Buffer overflow       | Missing capacity/length                | Pointer + capacity, checked copy    |
| OOB read              | Unbounded scan or bad index            | Length-bounded parsing              |
| Use-after-free        | Lifetime/ownership confusion           | Clear ownership and invalidation    |
| Double free           | Multiple owners                        | Single owner, move/release protocol |
| Memory leak           | Missing cleanup path                   | `goto cleanup`, destroy functions   |
| Signed overflow       | Unchecked arithmetic                   | Checked arithmetic                  |
| Unsigned underflow    | Size subtraction before ordering check | Check before subtracting            |
| Format mismatch       | Variadic type contract violation       | Warnings, exact formats             |
| String truncation bug | Ignored `snprintf` result              | Define truncation policy            |
| Struct padding leak   | Raw serialization                      | Field-wise serialization            |
| Strict aliasing bug   | Incompatible pointer punning           | `memcpy` or documented extension    |
| Misaligned access     | Cast to stricter type                  | Align or copy                       |
| Data race             | Unsynchronized shared access           | Locks/atomics                       |
| Deadlock              | Lock order/callback under lock         | Lock policy                         |
| ABI break             | Public layout/signature change         | Opaque/versioned API                |
| Header mismatch       | Manual duplicate declarations          | Single public header                |
| Link failure          | Missing dependency                     | Build-system target linkage         |
| Platform break        | Unlabeled POSIX/compiler feature       | Adapter/feature detection           |
| Parser bug            | Trusting external length/tag           | Validate before use                 |
| Cleanup bug           | Partial failure path untested          | Failure injection and sanitizers    |

### Final Comparison — C vs higher-level language expectations

| Higher-level expectation                 | C reality                                         |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| Arrays carry length                      | Pointer does not carry length                     |
| Strings are safe text objects            | C strings are null-terminated byte sequences      |
| Object lifetime follows references       | Pointers do not keep objects alive                |
| Assignment transfers or clones ownership | Assignment copies bits/members                    |
| Exceptions unwind resources              | C has no native unwinding cleanup                 |
| Types encode nullability/ownership       | C types mostly do not                             |
| Modules enforce privacy                  | Headers/linkage only partially enforce boundaries |
| Generics are type-safe                   | C genericity is macro/`void *`/codegen discipline |
| Closures capture safely                  | Function pointer + `userdata` is unchecked        |
| Threads see shared state safely          | Data races are UB                                 |
| Package manager handles dependencies     | C ecosystem is fragmented                         |
| ABI is automatic                         | ABI must be designed and preserved                |
| Runtime catches invalid memory           | UB may do anything or appear to work              |

### Final Decision Tables — choosing the right C mechanism

**Data representation:**

| Need                     | Prefer                              |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| Plain small value        | Public struct                       |
| Invariant-bearing object | Opaque type                         |
| Dynamic sequence         | `data/len/cap`                      |
| Borrowed sequence        | view/span                           |
| Optional result          | status + output or nullable pointer |
| Closed variant           | tagged union                        |
| Combinable options       | bitmask flags                       |
| Many options             | options struct                      |
| Same-lifetime objects    | arena                               |
| Shared ownership         | refcount only if necessary          |
| Public stable reference  | handle table                        |

**Behavior abstraction:**

| Need                           | Prefer                                                        |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Ordinary reusable behavior     | function                                                      |
| Small header helper            | `static inline`                                               |
| Runtime behavior customization | callback/function pointer                                     |
| Open backend interface         | ops table                                                     |
| Closed finite behavior         | enum + switch                                                 |
| Compile-time code generation   | macro or external generator                                   |
| Type-generic API               | `_Generic`, macro, `void *`, or generated code depending need |
| State transitions              | explicit state machine                                        |
| Parsing                        | cursor over pointer + length                                  |

**Resource and error handling:**

| Need                   | Prefer                     |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Caller-provided object | `init/deinit`              |
| API-allocated object   | `create/destroy`           |
| Multiple resources     | `goto cleanup`             |
| Fallible scalar result | status + output            |
| Rich diagnostics       | error object               |
| External validation    | runtime checks             |
| Internal invariant     | `assert` plus tests        |
| Ownership transfer     | `take/release/move`        |
| Cleanup grouping       | parent destructor or arena |

**Portability and tooling:**

| Need                      | Prefer                                  |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Cross-platform OS feature | adapter layer                           |
| Compiler extension        | guarded internal use                    |
| Stable ABI                | opaque/versioned API                    |
| Dependency integration    | target-based build config               |
| Memory bug detection      | ASan                                    |
| UB detection              | UBSan + review                          |
| Race detection            | TSan + design review                    |
| Parser robustness         | fuzzing                                 |
| Performance work          | profiler                                |
| Release confidence        | CI matrix and reproducible build policy |

### Final Mental Model — C as explicit contract engineering

C is powerful because it exposes representation, storage, control flow, linkage, and runtime boundaries directly. It is dangerous for the same reason. The language will not consistently prevent invalid lifetime, invalid bounds, invalid ownership, invalid aliasing, invalid arithmetic, invalid synchronization, or invalid ABI assumptions.

The mature C model is:

| Domain             | Expert interpretation                                                                        |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Pointer            | Address-like value plus unexpressed lifetime, extent, type, ownership, and aliasing contract |
| Array              | Contiguous objects with size known only where preserved                                      |
| String             | Convention over bytes, requiring terminator and encoding policy                              |
| Struct             | Representation plus possible invariant, not automatically protected                          |
| Union              | Storage overlay requiring active-member discipline                                           |
| Function           | Typed call boundary but incomplete effect contract                                           |
| Header             | Public source contract, not a module system                                                  |
| Translation unit   | Separate compilation boundary                                                                |
| Linkage            | Name binding across translation units                                                        |
| Library            | ABI/API contract plus dependency and build policy                                            |
| Error              | Explicit value/control path                                                                  |
| Resource           | Explicit acquire/release pair                                                                |
| Callback           | Unchecked behavioral boundary                                                                |
| Macro              | Token transformation before type checking                                                    |
| Undefined behavior | Absence of C semantics                                                                       |
| Toolchain          | Part of the correctness environment                                                          |

The compact final rule is:

**C programming is the art of making memory, ownership, errors, invariants, portability, and binary contracts explicit enough that humans and tools can audit them.**

Everything else in C follows from that.
